name: Blackstone 1554 36-Inch Outdoor Flat Top Gas Griddle Station
tag: Best Flat Top for Cooking for a Crowd

specs.Lid: Not included on base model (sold separately)

whatWorks: Massive 768 sq in cooking surface handles 30 burgers or a full breakfast spread simultaneously — four independently controlled heat zones make multi-dish cooking genuinely practical
Cold-rolled steel surface develops a natural non-stick seasoning over time; once seasoned, smash burgers, stir fry, and pancakes cook better than on any grill grate
Rear-channel grease management system (redesigned from earlier front-drip models) channels grease cleanly into a removable cup — cleanup is scrape, wipe, and oil
60,000 BTU output heats the full surface quickly and recovers fast after adding cold food — restaurant-style performance at a consumer price point
worthKnowing: Cold-rolled steel will rust if neglected — requires seasoning before first use and a thin oil coat after every cook; storing outdoors through a Canadian winter without a cover and indoor storage during freeze-thaw cycles leads to surface rust
Base model ships without a lid or wind guards; wind is a known performance killer (burners struggle to hold temp), and the lid/windscreen accessories add $60–100 to the total cost
At ~120 lbs it is not truly portable — the folding legs help with transport but two people are needed to move it safely
Rolled steel requires more diligent maintenance than cast iron or stainless: a single session left wet or uncovered can produce rust that needs to be ground off and re-seasoned
verdict: The Blackstone 36" is the definitive backyard flat top for anyone who cooks for groups — the cooking surface and heat output genuinely outperform most competitors at this price. Canadian owners should budget for a hard lid and cover and plan to bring the griddle top inside or to a dry garage for winter. If you can commit to the maintenance routine, this griddle becomes an outdoor kitchen workhorse for breakfast, smash burgers, stir fry, and everything in between. Those who want lower maintenance should consider a Traeger or Weber grill instead.

realBuyers.quote: Once you learn the seasoning routine it becomes second nature — scrape, wipe, thin layer of oil, done. The cooking surface after a year is genuinely non-stick.
Wind is the Achilles heel on the base model. Bought the wind guards after my first windy cook and it was a totally different experience.
Brought the top inside for the winter after reading horror stories about rust. Pulled it out in spring and it was perfect. Don't leave it outside in Canadian winters.

metaDescription: The Blackstone 36" griddle delivers 768 sq in of flat top cooking power for $479 CAD. Honest review covering seasoning, rust, Canadian winter storage, and who it's for.
introText: The Blackstone 36" Outdoor Flat Top Griddle is the best-selling propane griddle in North America, and for good reason: 768 square inches of cold-rolled steel cooking surface, four independently controlled burners, and enough output to feed a crowd. It's a different cooking experience than a grill — no flare-ups, no grates, just flat, even heat across the whole surface. Canadian owners should know going in that the steel surface requires consistent maintenance to keep rust away, especially through freeze-thaw winters.
